If you aren't in an evacuation zone, make sure you have plenty of water, batteries and emergency lights and keep your tub filled with water so you can flush your toilet, non perishables as well of course. Evacuation zones usually end up going to one of the local schools they open for shelter. Hotels are expensive and usually booked. They will supply food and cots, usually have generators running. You can find a list of schools and additional shelters with the evacuation zones on any of the news channels when the time gets closer.

welcome from Scotland, the homeland of my forefathers. I suggest asking the hotel front desk if they are in an evacuation zone, what are their storm plans, do they have emergency back up generators if power goes out, is there a grocery store close by for you to stock up on water and canned food, non perishable food items , a cooler, ice, batteries and flashlight,etc. Also recommend having all your electronic devices fully charged and a battery backup. Also ask hotel what local weather app you should download and keep an eye on the national Hurricane center, NOAA. Stay safe, from FL Native who ❤️ Scotland
